question: Can the no-helper DeepSeek/Qwen baselines be rerun with the Primus-Turbo external DeepEP backend?

answer.read: The retained no-helper rows were plain standard EP. The already-completed Primus-Turbo rows use the in-tree TurboEPBackend raw top-k path, not the optional external DeepEPBackend. A true external DEEP_EP run is blocked in the current images because the required deep_ep/deepep package is not installed.

turbo_ep_backend.status: already measured for no-helper raw top-k EP

primus_turbo_deepep_readme.read: Primus-Turbo marks DeepEP experimental and documents hardware support as MI300/gfx942 only.

primus_uep_bootstrap.read: USING_UEP requires both uccl and deep_ep packages before exporting PRIMUS_TURBO_MOE_DISPATCH_COMBINE_BACKEND=DEEP_EP.

remote_smokes.note: The import smoke was interrupted after package availability was printed because importing primus_turbo triggered unrelated AITER JIT compilation.
The import smoke was interrupted after package availability was printed because importing primus_turbo triggered unrelated AITER JIT compilation.
The Primus image exposes the DEEP_EP enum but does not include the required external backend package.

node_cleanup.do_sonle5_mi350_gpu: No diagnostic container left running; lighthouse container only.
node_cleanup.do_vunguyen_mi350_gpu: No diagnostic container left running after import smoke cleanup.

why: Current images do not have deep_ep/deepep, uccl, or rocshmem installed.
Primus-Turbo docs mark the experimental DeepEP path as MI300/gfx942-only, while the active nodes are MI350/gfx950.
Launching a 20-step training job with PRIMUS_TURBO_MOE_DISPATCH_COMBINE_BACKEND=DEEP_EP would fail at backend dependency selection, before producing a fair metric.
decision.next_action_if_still_desired: Build or pull a MI350-compatible image with external deep_ep/uccl/rocSHMEM installed, run a one-step DEEP_EP backend smoke first, then run the same 20-step no-helper MBS ladder.

related_measured_baseline.read: Use this as the current Primus-backed no-helper baseline; it is TurboEPBackend, not external DeepEPBackend.
